meaning: adj.人类学的,lly//adv.人类学上/人类学上的
examples: Ethnologists had a distinct reason for wanting to hear the stories: they were after linguistic or anthropological data that would supplement their own field observations.
Alan Macfarlane, professor of anthropological science at King's College, Cambridge has, like other historians, spent decades wrestling with the enigma of the Industrial Revolution.
Our definition of culture is much more anthropological.
Extensive anthropological and ethnographic research across India and in many countries around the world was completed.
The devices are the first instruments of their type in Italy devoted to anthropological research.
